articleTexts.title: Longmont Seeks Designs for Official City Flag - Longmont E2E Quality Retest
articleTexts.sample: Longmont Seeks Designs for Official City Flag - Longmont E2E Quality Retest Longmont E2E Quality Retest Cleanroom public beta issue for Longmont civic coverage. Home About Ethics Policy How We Report Corrections Ledger RSS Feed Longmont Seeks Designs for Official City Flag Published: 2026-06-29T15:33:30.835202900+00:00 Format: watch The city of Longmont is inviting residents to submit designs for its official flag as part of an inclusive community engagement process Source . Longmont has established a formal application process for interested individuals and organizations, ensuring that the design reflects the diverse culture and values of the city Source . The deadline for submissions is October 15, 2026. Interested parties can find detailed guidelines and submission forms on the City of Longmont’s official website. The flag will serve as a symbol of unity and pride among Longmont residents, enhancing community cohesion and visibility Source . Community members are encouraged to participate by visiting the city's website or contacting the Public Information Office for more information. articleTexts.sample: City Council Meeting Schedule Provides Insight into Ordinance Approval - Longmont E2E Quality Retest Longmont E2E Quality Retest Cleanroom public beta issue for Longmont civic coverage. Home About Ethics Policy How We Report Corrections Ledger RSS Feed City Council Meeting Schedule Provides Insight into Ordinance Approval Published: 2026-06-29T15:33:55.052874400+00:00 Format: watch The Longmont City Council schedules regular and special meetings throughout the month to address various issues affecting the community Source . These meetings play a crucial role in the approval process of ordinances and resolutions, which are key tools for changing local laws. During these sessions, residents can provide input through the Public Invited to be Heard segment, where their voices can influence decision-making. According to the source, Council members often direct staff to prepare an ordinance based on community feedback received during these meetings Source . This process ensures that local laws reflect the concerns and needs of the community.
